userStories.title: 9.1.10 — Decompose Effects into thin resolver calls
userStories.description:
Tests: unit + integration — Verify Effects are thin orchestrators and pipeline handles surrounding concerns.
Move responsibilities out of Effect classes (AttackEffect, future ShootEffect, CastEffect) and into the action framework pipeline built in MFG-51.
Blocked by: MFG-50 (unified buffs map), MFG-51 (action framework rework)
Current state:
- AttackEffect.execute() is a God method: target validation, adjacency check, defensive reaction prompts, bead spending, entity state reads/writes, resolver call, animation event generation — all in one method
- Effects require full GameContext to test (grid, entities, adapter, beads) making them hard to unit test
- Each new action type (shoot, cast) would duplicate this pattern with its own God method
- Pure resolvers (resolveAttack, resolveRangedAttack, resolveSpellCast) already exist and are trivially testable — but Effects wrap them with 100+ lines of orchestration
Desired state:
- Target validation (adjacency, range) handled during parameter collection in the pipeline
- Payment (beads, prep stacks) handled by the pipeline before effect execution (MFG-51)
- Defensive reactions handled as a pipeline-level hook when an entity is targeted by an attack/spell
- State mutation (apply damage, modify guard/evasion) handled by the pipeline after effect returns its result
- Effects become thin: take pre-resolved inputs, call resolver, return result and animation descriptors
- Effects are essentially resolver calls with minimal glue — potentially just the resolvers themselves
- New effect types (ShootEffect, CastEffect) are trivial to implement since the pipeline handles the complex orchestration
Acceptance Criteria:
- AttackEffect slimmed to resolver call + animation event generation (no target lookup, no adjacency check, no defensive reaction prompt, no bead spending, no direct entity mutation)
- Pipeline handles targeting validation, defensive reactions, and state mutation
- ShootEffect created as thin wrapper around resolveRangedAttack()
- CastEffect created as thin wrapper around resolveSpellCast() + resolveMagicalEffect()
- All existing tests pass (combat-resolver, effects, defensive-reactions, action-resolution)
- Unit tests: minimum 6 new scenarios for pipeline hooks (targeting, reactions, state mutation)

userStories.title: 9.1.9 — Rework action framework for modifiers and flexible payment
userStories.description:
Tests: unit + integration — Action resolution pipeline with modifier declaration, payment, and execution.
Rework the action framework to support the modifier and payment flexibility required by MFG-8 (Universal Actions) and MFG-9 (Weapons).
Current gaps (from MFG-7-explanations.md):
1. Bead payment never executed — Actions declare bead costs in ActionCost but the pipeline never calls beadHand.spend(). Only defensive reactions actually spend beads.
2. No "bead OR prep stack" payment — ActionCost only supports fixed bead costs. No way to declare alternative payment (e.g., "1 Red bead OR 1 Windup stack").
3. Option modifiers broken in new pipeline — ActionResolution.applyEffects() passes {} for modifiers. Modifier merging only works in the legacy path.
4. No weapon category filter on modifiers — Can't restrict a modifier to "light weapons only" or "two-handed only".
5. No conditional modifiers — Can't express "only available if target has guard=0 AND evasion=0" (needed for Percer).
6. No prep stack consumption in action flow — Nothing connects PreparationManager.consumeStacks() to action execution.
7. UnifiedAttackResolver exists unnecessarily — Stat composition (base + weapon) should happen in the action layer, not a separate resolver. Delete src/combat/UnifiedAttackResolver.ts.
Desired state:
- Action resolution pipeline automatically deducts bead costs before effect execution
- New PaymentOption type supports alternative currencies: { options: [{ red: 1 }, { prepStack: 'windup' }] } — player chooses which to pay
- Modifier application works in the new ActionResolution pipeline (port from legacy)
- Modifiers can declare eligibility predicates (weapon category, target state conditions)
- Weapon stat composition (base P1/A1 + weapon power/agility) done in action/effect layer, not a separate resolver
- Preparation stacks consumed as part of payment flow when used as currency
- UnifiedAttackResolver.ts deleted; its tests rewritten to test the action framework directly
Acceptance Criteria:
- Bead costs are automatically paid when action executes (not just UI check)
- At least one action supports "bead OR prep stack" alternative payment
- Option modifiers apply correctly in new ActionResolution pipeline
- UnifiedAttackResolver.ts deleted, unified-attack.feature rewritten
- Existing action-resolution tests pass
- Unit + integration tests: minimum 8 new scenarios

userStories.title: 9.1.8 — Unify status effect and preparation storage on Entity
userStories.description:
Tests: unit only — Migrate storage from external managers to entity-owned map. Existing tests must continue to pass.
Move all status effect and preparation stack storage from standalone manager singletons to a single Map<string, number> on the Entity class ("character data sheet" model).
Current state:
- StatusEffectManager owns a private Map<string, number> for burn stacks — external to the entity
- PreparationManager owns a private Map<string, Map<PreparationType, number>> — also external
- Adding new effects (bleed, poison, etc.) requires adding new private maps and dedicated methods
- Entity state is fragmented across multiple singletons
Desired state:
- Entity gets a buffs: Map<string, number> (key = effect name like "burn", "bleed", "windup", "aim"; value = stack count)
- Generic methods on Entity: addStacks(name, count), getStacks(name), clearStacks(name), clearAll()
- PreparationManager becomes a rule enforcer (maxStacks, interruption logic, pairedActions) that reads/writes the entity's map
- StatusEffectManager becomes a rule enforcer (end-of-round damage, consumption) that reads/writes the entity's map
- New status effects (bleed, poison) only need a resolution rule — no new maps or dedicated methods
- Character sheet rendering: just iterate the entity's buffs map
Acceptance Criteria:
- Entity has a unified buffs map with addStacks/getStacks/clearStacks/clearAll methods
- PreparationManager delegates storage to Entity.buffs, retains rule logic (maxStacks, interruption, pairedActions)
- StatusEffectManager delegates storage to Entity.buffs, retains resolution logic (end-of-round damage)
- All existing preparation tests (17 scenarios) pass
- All existing status-effects tests (10 scenarios) pass
- Unit tests: minimum 5 new scenarios for the unified buffs map on Entity
